channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: Claude + NotebookLM: Ultimate AI Automation Workflow to Work 10x Faster
item.description: AI Master: A no-code AI workflow combining Claude with NotebookLM for automated content creation. Three workflows are presented: converting topics and sources into multiple content formats (YouTube script, podcast, slides, infographic); using Claude to engineer prompts for generating 4K infographics from PDFs; and automating NotebookLM tasks via Claude Cowork and browser extensions.

item.title: Why Italians Saw the Pope Very Differently - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: This is a historical commentary by Ada Palmer on how Italian perspectives on papal authority differed from other regions. The discussion explores divergent views and cultural contexts around religious leadership in Italian history.

item.title: Agent identity: A new access model for Claude Tag
item.description: Claude: Claude Tag introduces an agent identity model that allows Claude to operate autonomously in Slack channels under its own scoped identity rather than impersonating users. The system is designed for safety in multiplayer sessions, with admin governance and full auditability across different workspaces.

item.title: Tag Claude in, right where you already work
item.description: Claude: Anthropic introduced Claude Tag, allowing users to invoke Claude directly within Slack by tagging @Claude in threads. The feature maintains channel context, integrates with organizational tools, and persists work across multiple days without requiring recontextualization. Currently available in beta on Team and Enterprise plans.

item.title: GPT explained visually..
item.description: Caleb Writes Code: A video by Caleb Writes Code providing a visual walkthrough of how GPT models work under the hood. The video covers core transformer components including self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, token embeddings, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections.

item.title: Converting T-SQL code to PL/pgSQL with confidence
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud's Gemini AI serves as a tutor for converting T-SQL database code to PL/pgSQL. The tool explains conversion logic, including refactoring procedures into Common Table Expressions, to help developers migrate applications to PostgreSQL on Google Cloud.

item.title: When millions of AI agents meet
item.description: Google DeepMind: Nenad Tomašev discusses the emerging concept of an agentic economy where millions of autonomous AI agents transact and negotiate with one another. The conversation explores operational shifts from single systems to distributed intelligence, including psychological risks like automation bias and cybersecurity challenges such as dynamic cloaking and agentic traps.

item.title: How Anthropic Employees ACTUALLY Use Claude Skills
item.description: Austin Marchese: Austin Marchese presents five lessons on how Anthropic employees use Claude Skills, extracted from Anthropic's published materials. He argues that Skills are more than markdown files and should be built as a compounding system, covering categories, power components, verifiers, gotchas, and triggers.

item.title: AI Resume Builder — How to Automate Job Applications & Resume Writing
item.description: AI Master: Jobright is an AI resume builder platform used by 2M+ people to automate job applications, resume tailoring, role matching, and cover letter generation. The platform includes features like application tracking, insider referral finding, and an AI career coach.

item.title: Sakana Fugu (Fully Tested - V/S Fable): UHM... REALLY?
item.description: AICodeKing: Sakana Fugu is a multi-agent orchestration system that routes requests across models. It comes in two versions: Fugu and Fugu Ultra. While Fugu Ultra performs well on benchmarks like Terminal Bench and LiveCodeBench, it underperforms Fable 5 on SWE Bench Pro and Humanity's Last Exam. Hidden orchestration costs and weak practical performance on coding tasks limit its current viability as a direct Fable replacement.

item.title: AI Agents VS Basic Chatbots: The FUTURE is HERE! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: The video argues that AI agents like Claude outperform traditional RAG chatbots on complex tasks such as file management, spreadsheet creation, and communications. It frames this capability gap as representing the future direction of AI productivity tools.

item.title: Anthropic vs. Normal Companies: Speed of Innovation Revealed! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof argues that nimble startups like Anthropic outpace large corporations through organizational agility rather than just innovation capability. The advantage stems from lack of legacy systems and rigid employee structures that slow down larger firms.

item.title: AI's BIGGEST Problem: The Adoption Gap Explained! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: The adoption gap in AI is identified as a major problem where cutting-edge systems like agentic AI and LLMs advance rapidly but businesses struggle to integrate them. The gap is widening, leaving many companies behind in deployment.

item.title: Local AI vs Cloud: Why Your Excel Data Shouldn't Go To Azure! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof argues that sensitive data in Excel should remain on-premises rather than sent to cloud services like Azure for AI integration. The recommendation emphasizes privacy preservation and risk avoidance through local model deployment.

item.title: NEW Claude Design Update Changes EVERYTHING!
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Julian Goldie SEO discusses a design update to Claude, claiming it changes everything. The video promotes AI tools and courses for SEO and business automation.

item.title: This NEW Hermes Update Builds Agents From Scratch
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Hermes introduces a Blank Slate mode that lets users build AI agents from scratch by starting with minimal components and adding only necessary tools. This approach reduces bloat, prevents unwanted feature drift from updates, and simplifies agent configuration for lean use cases.

item.title: OpenAI's Secret: Why Compute Power Fuels AI Success! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: The video argues that OpenAI's early investment in data center infrastructure and compute power was central to their AI breakthroughs. The author asserts that compute infrastructure is the critical factor in achieving AI success.

item.title: Closer to the Material, Ryo Lu | Compile 26
item.description: Cursor: Ryo Lu discusses how AI is changing software development practices and material interaction. He explores the tension between AI abstraction and maintaining transparency, craftsmanship, and connection to the building process itself.

item.title: This NEW NotebookLM Update Changes Everything
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Google's NotebookLM has been upgraded to function as a full AI agent capable of writing code, building sources, and delivering finished outputs autonomously. New features include a cloud computer for handling code and documents, auto-source building from initial ideas, and a Studio for generating podcasts and reports with cited answers.

item.title: Why Hiring More Won't Make Your AI Faster! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof argues that hiring additional staff may be counterproductive when AI tools can automate coding and streamline operations. The video suggests companies should reconsider growth strategy given AI's capacity to reduce expenses.

item.title: Gemma Playground: Parallel Agents in Action
item.description: Google for Developers: Ian Ballantyne demonstrates Gemma 4's ability to run 10 parallel independent agents on a single local machine, orchestrating work through a delegator and generating SVG art galleries at over 170 tokens per second. The demonstration covers local batch processing, concurrent multi-agent task delegation, and use cases for enterprise and private on-premises deployments.

item.title: Claude Code AI SEO: How to Rank #1 with Claude Code
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Julian Goldie presents an agentic SEO system that integrates Claude Code with a user's own Google Search Console data to identify rankable keywords and automatically generate and deploy SEO content across multiple sites. The approach bypasses common keyword tools to find less competitive opportunities, with a demonstrated case of increasing site traffic from 2 to 200+ daily clicks.

item.title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu - Chapter 7 - Moving (live, unedited)
item.description: CS50: CS50 is Harvard University's introduction to computer science course that includes a section on artificial intelligence taught by Brian Yu. The course is available through edX, Harvard Extension School, Harvard Summer School, and OpenCourseWare, with supporting communities on Discord, GitHub, Reddit, and other platforms.

item.title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu - Chapter 6 - Generating (live, unedited)
item.description: CS50: CS50 is Harvard University's introductory computer science course. This video is Chapter 6 on "Generating" from the Introduction to Artificial Intelligence course taught by Brian Yu and David J. Malan. The course is available through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and OpenCourseWare.

item.title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu - Chapter 5 - Communicating (live, unedited)
item.description: CS50: CS50's Introduction to Artificial Intelligence Chapter 5 covers communicating concepts and techniques in AI. The course is offered through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and Harvard Summer School. It is led by Brian Yu with coordination by David J. Malan.

item.title: Run Powerful AI Locally: The Future is 18 Months Away! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof predicts that small, open-source language models will run locally on consumer hardware within 18 months to 2 years. This shift would reduce cloud dependence and decentralize AI processing.

item.title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu - Chapter 4 - Sensing (live, unedited)
item.description: CS50: CS50 is Harvard University's foundational computer science course covering intellectual enterprises and programming. Chapter 4 focuses on Sensing. The course is offered through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and OpenCourseWare with extensive community engagement channels.

item.title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu - Chapter 3 - Analyzing (live, unedited)
item.description: CS50: CS50 is Harvard University's introduction to computer science and artificial intelligence course taught by David J. Malan. The course is available through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, Harvard Summer School, and OpenCourseWare, with an active community across Discord, Slack, Reddit, and other social channels.

item.title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu - Chapter 2 - Predicting (live, unedited)
item.description: CS50: CS50 is Harvard University's foundational computer science course offered through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and Harvard Summer School. This specific video is Chapter 2 on Predicting, taught by Brian Yu as part of the AI module, presented as live unedited content.

item.title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu - Chapter 1 - Playing (live, unedited)
item.description: CS50: CS50's Introduction to Artificial Intelligence is Harvard's foundational course on AI concepts, taught by Brian Yu. The course covers core AI topics and is available through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and OpenCourseWare with live unedited lecture content distributed on YouTube.

item.title: Opening Keynote, Michael Truell | Compile 26
item.description: Cursor: At Compile 26, Cursor announced Cursor Mobile, Origin Git platform for agents, and a new model trained from scratch. The company reports 95% of usage is now agent-based and is moving toward an agent-first architecture with dedicated compute resources.

item.title: NEW Hermes Agent Updates Change Everything!
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Hermes 0.17 introduces a framework for building teams of autonomous AI agents with async sub-agents, loop engineering for quality control, and an Agent Operating System. The update enables no-code automation and business content automation at scale.

item.title: Big Tech's AI Cost Advantage: Scale Wins! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Large tech corporations have a cost advantage in AI adoption due to massive scale and high resource utilization. They can repurpose existing infrastructure to achieve efficient AI transformation.

item.title: NEW AI SEO Agent is INSANE!
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Julian Goldie promotes a new AI SEO agent designed for automating search engine optimization tasks. The agent is positioned as a powerful tool integrated with Agent OS and promoted through his AI Profit Lab community.

item.title: Rethinking the ROI of cybersecurity
item.description: IBM Technology: IBM Technology argues that security spending should not impede innovation. The video advocates for strategic risk thinking that balances security protection with business growth and speed to market.

item.title: Are AI Glasses Over? + Big Technology AI Summit Audience Questions
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: A podcast episode featuring Ranjan Roy and Alex Kantrowitz discussing the viability of AR glasses (with reference to Snapchat Specs), optimal form factors for AI devices, applications in biology, US-China AI relations, and corporate responsibility in AI development. The episode covers multiple audience questions from the Big Technology AI Summit.

item.title: Gemini 3.5 Pro LEAKS is INSANE!
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Julian Goldie SEO discusses leaked features of Gemini 3.5 Pro, characterizing them as remarkable. The video promotes his AI-related courses and services including Agent OS and AI SEO strategies.

item.title: Get Fable 5 output on a cheaper model
item.description: Jack Roberts: A leaked Fable 5 system prompt can be injected into Claude Opus 4.8 to achieve near-identical output quality at a fraction of the cost. The approach requires only one line of code and is available on GitHub.

item.title: Businesses Risk Losing AI Race: The Agentic Adoption Gap! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof argues that businesses delaying agentic AI adoption face a widening competitive gap. He identifies hardware and foundational building blocks as critical factors for organizations to remain competitive in the AI era.

item.title: Google’s new AI agent stack from I/O 2026
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google announced a new AI agent stack at I/O 2026 featuring the Gemini 3.5 Flash model, Agent Development Kit 2.0 with graph-based orchestration, the Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol for standardized agent communication, and the Managed Agents API. The stack includes developer tools like agent CLI, enterprise skill registries, and the Gemini Omni Flash multimodal video model.

item.title: Bring Gemini’s intelligence to AlloyDB using AI functions
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: AlloyDB introduces AI functions that integrate Gemini directly into SQL workflows. Functions include ai.generate, ai.rank, ai.if, ai.forecast, ai.summarize, ai.agg_summarize, and ai.analyze_sentiment. The optimized implementation provides significant performance improvements and cost reduction compared to row-at-a-time LLM calls.

item.title: AI Location Control: Give Clients FULL Power! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof discusses how clients should have full control over AI operations and geographical deployment decisions. The piece emphasizes that complete control over artificial intelligence infrastructure is critical for proper client governance.

item.title: Sakana Fugu vs Fusion (Beats Fable 5?)
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Julian Goldie compares Sakana's Fugu (Ultra and Mini) with OpenRouter Fusion via side-by-side tests on visual and code generation tasks. Fugu Ultra ranks first for quality, Fusion second, and Fugu Mini third, though Fugu offers cheaper flat pricing with daily caps while Fusion charges per token with easier API access.

item.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
item.description: EO: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that code is only one component of a software moat; competitive advantage comes from customer context, proprietary data, product experience, and operational scale. He contends that concerns about AI eliminating SaaS companies are overstated and that the best AI products build passionate customer bases rather than mere satisfaction.

item.title: Learn 97% of Claude in Under 12 Minutes
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts walks through seven levels of Claude functionality, from basic chat to autonomous agents and code generation. Coverage includes model selection between Opus and Sonnet, prompt engineering, integrations with tools like Firecrawl and Gmail, sub-agents for parallel task execution, codebase analysis with Graphify, and the Claude Code operating system for spend tracking.

item.title: What caused this React app to crash?
item.description: Google for Developers: Google for Developers presents a CI pipeline debugging puzzle where a React app crashes after upgrading from React 18 to React 19. The build runs faster than expected and the production failure is traced to a cache configuration issue, highlighting a real-world build-system pattern.

item.title: Anthropic's AI Future: GPUs, Power & Unseen Security! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: A YouTube short discussing Anthropic's AI infrastructure, focusing on GPU costs, power requirements, and security mechanisms like sandboxes and MCP tunnels. The video explores the operational infrastructure needed to support modern AI systems.

item.title: NEW Hermes Agent v0.17.0 is INSANE
item.description: Julian Goldie SEO: Hermes Agent v0.17.0, called "The Reach Release," is an open-source agent framework that integrates across applications with a new Blank Slate setup mode that lets users start with minimal configuration and add only needed features. The release includes iMessage integration, background sub-agents, persistent memory, and desktop upgrades.

item.title: What it takes to build *realtime* voice models | Voxtral
item.description: Julia Turc: Julia Turc examines Mistral's Voxtral Realtime and Voxtral TTS as examples of modern realtime voice model architecture. The video covers modular vs end-to-end speech design, audio tokenization techniques including vector quantization and finite scalar quantization, and key historical milestones like Whisper, Whisper Streaming, and WaveNet.

item.title: AI Agents: DEAD or ALIVE? Businesses Are Still Waiting! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Joao argues that advanced AI agents with sophisticated capabilities already exist today. However, businesses remain hesitant and slow to adopt these tools in practice, creating a gap between technological availability and enterprise deployment.
